notes: v1 starter set: 5 real, freely-licensed government PDF forms fetched directly over HTTPS from irs.gov / uscis.gov (see source_url per row). This is NOT the >=500-document target from P0-08's original acceptance criteria - that gap is tracked in tasks/escalations/E-005-corpus-acquisition-gap.md and docs/specs/corpus-plan.md.

page_count and text_sha256 were computed once at authoring time using Apple PDFKit (a system framework) as an inspection tool only - this is NOT PDFium and is NOT part of DocEngineHost/the shipping app; it is the equivalent of opening the file in Preview.app to record its facts, formalized as a script for reproducibility. See Fixtures/pdf-corpus/README.md.

render_checksum is intentionally omitted: DocEngineHost/PDFium (P0-06) now exists, but corpus-open has not yet been wired to open documents through it and compute a render checksum - that's separate follow-up scope (see Scripts/bench.sh's render-latency suite for the P0-06 tile-render bench that IS wired up). Scripts/bench.sh corpus-open still validates only file presence and sha256 integrity today.
file_sha256 is the raw-file hash and is engine-independent - it detects corruption/tampering of the fixture itself and needs no PDF engine to check.

rows.license_note: US federal government work (IRS form) - public domain, no licensing restriction on redistribution.
rows.text_hash_method: sha256 of PDFKit PDFPage.string per page, newline-joined, authoring-time reference value

malformed_rows.derived_from: starter/irs-f4506t.pdf (every 7th byte in the first 5000 bytes after the header, offset 9 onward, XORed with 0xFF)
malformed_rows.expected_behavior: PDFium's built-in xref-rebuild repair heuristic (a full-file scan for 'N 0 obj' markers, used when the declared xref table doesn't parse) recovers this file despite the corruption, since most 'obj'/'endobj' tokens survive a sparse every-7th-byte flip - open() succeeds. This is a real, useful finding (PDFKit at authoring time was stricter and would have rejected the same bytes) documented here rather than fought with denser corruption, which would just degenerate into duplicating truncated-header-only's coverage. No crash either way is what's actually asserted.
malformed_rows.verified_rejected_by: N/A - opens successfully; see DocEngineHost PDFiumEngine (P0-06) PDFiumEngineTests.testOpenGracefullyRejectsEachMalformedFixture's allowlist for this id

malformed_rows.derived_from: starter/irs-fw4.pdf (every 3rd byte in the last 20% of the file XORed with 0xAA, targeting the xref/trailer region)
malformed_rows.expected_behavior: reject: typed error, no crash, no partial write
